content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactual Chain-of-Thought Prompting to Mitigate LLM Hallucination",
  "problem": "Current LLMs frequently produce confident but hallucinated facts, even when prompted with chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning. Existing prompting methods rarely interrogate or contrast the model's own outputs.",
  "motivation": "Hallucination undermines LLM reliability for factual tasks and knowledge-intensive applications. Recent work focuses mostly on positive-only CoT elaborations, overlooking how exposure to model-generated counterfactuals could calibrate factuality. As models scale, new prompting strategies are needed to push factual grounding without external retrieval.",
  "method": "We propose 'Counterfactual Chain-of-Thought Prompting' (CF-CoT): given an initial question, the prompt requests the model to first generate a plausible-but-incorrect fact chain, then immediately generate a contrasting chain that corrects and justifies the errors. This contrastive self-interrogation leverages the model's generative capacity to surface and correct plausible hallucinations within a single prompt, explicitly conditioning the final answer on the correction step. The prompt template can be applied to factual QA, summarization, or open-ended generation tasks.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate CF-CoT on TruthfulQA, HotpotQA, and the HallucinationEval datasets, comparing against standard CoT and direct-answer prompts. Metrics include factuality (via manual annotation and automated fact-checking), answer accuracy, and hallucination rate. Ablations will test the impact of counterfactual ordering and model size."
}
